heading: Loan guarantees for Indian housing
label: 1715z–13a Loan guarantees for Indian housing

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992, and not as part of the National Housing Act which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2020—Subsec. (c)(5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 116–2602014—Subsec. (h)(1)(B). inserted after first sentence “Exhausting all reasonable possibilities of collection by the holder of the guarantee shall include a good faith consideration of loan modification as well as meeting standards for servicing loans in default, as determined by the Secretary.”Pub. L. 113–2352013—Subsec. (d). amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary shall fix and collect a guarantee fee for the guarantee of loans under this section, which may not exceed the amount equal to 1 percent of the principal obligation of the loan. The fee shall be paid by the lender at time of issuance of the guarantee and shall be adequate, in the determination of the Secretary, to cover expenses and probable losses. The Secretary shall deposit any fees collected under this subsection in the Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund established under subsection (i) of this section.”Pub. L. 113–62007—Subsec. (i)(5)(C),(7). substituted “fiscal years 2008 through 2012” for “fiscal years 1997 through 2007”.Pub. L. 110–372002—Subsec. Findings Related to Improvements to Loan Guarantees for Indian Housing, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. Q, title I, § 105(a)134 Stat. 2170Dec. 27, 2020“Congress finds that—“(1)the extended timelines for approving lenders’ applications to participate in the program established under section 184 of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 () are unacceptably long;12 U.S.C. 1715z–13a“(2)those extended timelines inhibit the ability of lenders to provide needed mortgage loans on Native American reservations; and“(3)it can take a significant amount of time for certain Bureau of Indian Affairs Land Title and Records Offices to issue final certified title status reports for mortgages issued on Indian trust land under section 184 of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 (), which delays the guarantee of the loan by the Department of Housing and Urban Development.”12 U.S.C. 1715z–13a

Reports on Acceleration of Processing, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. Q, title I, § 105(c)134 Stat. 2171Dec. 27, 2020“The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development shall—“(1)report to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives on a semi-annual basis on the progress that the Secretary is making to accelerate the processing of loan applications on fee simple and Indian trust land under section 184 of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 (); and12 U.S.C. 1715z–13a“(2)if there is no improvement in accelerating those processing timelines, submit to the committees described in paragraph (1) a report explaining the lack of improvement.”

children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: To provide access to sources of private financing to Indian families, Indian housing authorities, and Indian tribes, who otherwise could not acquire housing financing because of the unique legal status of Indian lands, the Secretary may guarantee not to exceed 100 percent of the unpaid principal and interest due on any loan eligible under subsection (b) made to an Indian family, Indian housing authority, or Indian tribe.

children.heading: Eligible borrowers
children.label: 1 Eligible borrowers
children.text: The loans shall be made only to borrowers who are Indian families, Indian housing authorities, or Indian tribes.

children.heading: Eligible housing
children.label: 2 Eligible housing
children.text: The loan shall be used to construct, acquire, refinance, or rehabilitate 1- to 4-family dwellings that are standard housing and are located on trust land or land located in an Indian or Alaska Native area.

children.heading: Security
children.label: 3 Security
children.text: The loan may be secured by any collateral authorized under existing Federal law or applicable State or tribal law.

children.label: A
children.text: Any mortgagee approved by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development for participation in the single family mortgage insurance program under title II of the National Housing Act [ et seq.].12 U.S.C. 1707

children.label: B
children.text: Any lender whose housing loans under chapter 37 of title 38 are automatically guaranteed pursuant to section 1802(d) of such title.1So in original. Probably should be section “3702(d)”.1

children.label: C
children.text: Any lender approved by the Secretary of Agriculture to make guaranteed loans for single family housing under the Housing Act of 1949 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1441

children.label: D
children.text: Any other lender that is supervised, approved, regulated, or insured by any agency of the Federal Government.

children.label: A
children.text: be made for a term not exceeding 30 years;

children.label: B
children.text: bear interest (exclusive of the guarantee fee under section 404 and service charges, if any) at a rate agreed upon by the borrower and the lender and determined by the Secretary to be reasonable, which may not exceed the rate generally charged in the area (as determined by the Secretary) for home mortgage loans not guaranteed or insured by any agency or instrumentality of the Federal Government;2So in original. Probably should be “subsection (d)”.2

children.label: i
children.text: 97.75 percent of the appraised value of the property as of the date the loan is accepted for guarantee (or 98.75 percent if the value of the property is $50,000 or less); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount approved by the Secretary under this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: involve a payment on account of the property (i) in cash or its equivalent, or (ii) through the value of any improvements to the property made through the skilled or unskilled labor of the borrower, as the Secretary shall provide.

children.heading: Approval process
children.label: 1 Approval process
children.text: Before the Secretary approves any loan for guarantee under this section, the lender shall submit the application for the loan to the Secretary for examination. If the Secretary approves the loan for guarantee, the Secretary shall issue a certificate under this paragraph as evidence of the guarantee.

children.heading: Standard for approval
children.label: 2 Standard for approval
children.text: The Secretary may approve a loan for guarantee under this section and issue a certificate under this paragraph only if the Secretary determines there is a reasonable prospect of repayment of the loan.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 3 Effect
children.text: A certificate of guarantee issued under this paragraph by the Secretary shall be conclusive evidence of the eligibility of the loan for guarantee under the provisions of this section and the amount of such guarantee. Such evidence shall be incontestable in the hands of the bearer and the full faith and credit of the United States is pledged to the payment of all amounts agreed to be paid by the Secretary as security for such obligations.

children.heading: Fraud and misrepresentation
children.label: 4 Fraud and misrepresentation
children.text: This subsection may not be construed to preclude the Secretary from establishing defenses against the original lender based on fraud or material misrepresentation or to bar the Secretary from establishing by regulations in effect on the date of issuance or disbursement, whichever is earlier, partial defenses to the amount payable on the guarantee.

children.label: i
children.text: a claim payment is presented to the Secretary due to the default of the borrower on the loan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the required trailing documents are outstanding.

children.heading: Termination of indemnification agreement
children.label: B Termination of indemnification agreement
children.text: An indemnification agreement between an originating lender and the Secretary described in subparagraph (A) shall only terminate upon receipt by the Secretary of the trailing documents described in that subparagraph in a form and manner that is acceptable to the Secretary.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as authorizing the Bureau of Indian Affairs to delay the issuance of a final certified title status report and recorded mortgage relating to a loan closed on Indian trust land.

children.heading: Guarantee fee
children.label: d Guarantee fee
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and collect, at the time of issuance of the guarantee, a fee for the guarantee of loans under this section, in an amount not exceeding 3 percent of the principal obligation of the loan. The Secretary may also establish and collect annual premium payments in an amount not exceeding 1 percent of the remaining guaranteed balance (excluding the portion of the remaining balance attributable to the fee collected at the time of issuance of the guarantee). The Secretary shall establish the amount of the fees and premiums by publishing a notice in the Federal Register. The Secretary shall deposit any fees and premiums collected under this subsection in the Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund established under subsection (i).

children.heading: Liability under guarantee
children.label: e Liability under guarantee
children.text: The liability under a guarantee provided under this section shall decrease or increase on a pro rata basis according to any decrease or increase in the amount of the unpaid obligation under the provisions of the loan agreement.

children.heading: Transfer and assumption
children.label: f Transfer and assumption
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any loan guaranteed under this section, including the security given for the loan, may be sold or assigned by the lender to any financial institution subject to examination and supervision by an agency of the Federal Government or of any State or the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Disqualification of lenders and civil money penalties
children.label: g Disqualification of lenders and civil money penalties
A
children.text: refuse, either temporarily or permanently, to guarantee any further loans made by such lender or holder;

children.label: B
children.text: bar such lender or holder from acquiring additional loans guaranteed under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: require that such lender or holder assume not less than 10 percent of any loss on further loans made or held by the lender or holder that are guaranteed under this section.

children.heading: Civil money penalties for intentional violations
children.label: 2 Civil money penalties for intentional violations
children.text: If the Secretary determines that any lender or holder of a guarantee certificate under subsection (c) has intentionally failed to maintain adequate accounting records, to adequately service loans guaranteed under this section, or to exercise proper credit or underwriting judgment, the Secretary may impose a civil money penalty on such lender or holder in the manner and amount provided under section 536 of the National Housing Act [] with respect to mortgagees and lenders under such Act.12 U.S.C. 1735f–14

children.heading: Payment on loans made in good faith
children.label: 3 Payment on loans made in good faith
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), the Secretary may not refuse to pay pursuant to a valid guarantee on loans of a lender or holder barred under this subsection if the loans were previously made in good faith.

children.heading: Foreclosure
children.label: i Foreclosure
children.text: The holder of the certificate may initiate foreclosure proceedings (after providing written notice of such action to the Secretary) and upon a final order by the court authorizing foreclosure and submission to the Secretary of a claim for payment under the guarantee, the Secretary shall pay to the holder of the certificate the pro rata portion of the amount guaranteed (as determined pursuant to subsection (e)) plus reasonable fees and expenses as approved by the Secretary. The Secretary shall be subrogated to the rights of the holder of the guarantee and the lender holder shall assign the obligation and security to the Secretary.

children.heading: No foreclosure
children.label: ii No foreclosure
children.text: Without seeking foreclosure (or in any case in which a foreclosure proceeding initiated under clause (i) continues for a period in excess of 1 year), the holder of the guarantee may submit to the Secretary a request to assign the obligation and security interest to the Secretary in return for payment of the claim under the guarantee. The Secretary may accept assignment of the loan if the Secretary determines that the assignment is in the best interests of the United States. Upon assignment, the Secretary shall pay to the holder of the guarantee the pro rata portion of the amount guaranteed (as determined under subsection (e)). The Secretary shall be subrogated to the rights of the holder of the guarantee and the holder shall assign the obligation and security to the Secretary.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: B Requirements
children.text: Before any payment under a guarantee is made under subparagraph (A), the holder of the guarantee shall exhaust all reasonable possibilities of collection. Exhausting all reasonable possibilities of collection by the holder of the guarantee shall include a good faith consideration of loan modification as well as meeting standards for servicing loans in default, as determined by the Secretary. Upon payment, in whole or in part, to the holder, the note or judgment evidencing the debt shall be assigned to the United States and the holder shall have no further claim against the borrower or the United States. The Secretary shall then take such action to collect as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Limitations on liquidation
children.label: 2 Limitations on liquidation
children.text: In the event of a default by the borrower on a loan guaranteed under this section involving a security interest in restricted Indian land, the mortgagee or the Secretary shall only pursue liquidation after offering to transfer the account to an eligible tribal member, the tribe, or the Indian housing authority serving the tribe or tribes. If the mortgagee or the Secretary subsequently proceeds to liquidate the account, the mortgagee or the Secretary shall not sell, transfer, or otherwise dispose of or alienate the property except to one of the entities described in the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund
children.label: i Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States the Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund for the purpose of providing loan guarantees under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: any amounts, claims, notes, mortgages, contracts, and property acquired by the Secretary under this section, and any collections and proceeds therefrom;

children.label: B
children.text: any amounts appropriated under paragraph (7);

children.label: C
children.text: any guarantee fees collected under subsection (d); and

children.label: D
children.text: any interest or earnings on amounts invested under paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: fulfilling any obligations of the Secretary with respect to loans guaranteed under this section, including the costs (as such term is defined in ) of such loans;section 661a of title 2

children.label: B
children.text: paying taxes, insurance, prior liens, expenses necessary to make fiscal adjustment in connection with the application and transmittal of collections, and other expenses and advances to protect the Secretary for loans which are guaranteed under this section or held by the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: acquiring such security property at foreclosure sales or otherwise;

children.label: D
children.text: paying administrative expenses in connection with this section; and

children.label: E
children.text: reasonable and necessary costs of rehabilitation and repair to properties that the Secretary holds or owns pursuant to this section.

children.heading: Investment
children.label: 4 Investment
children.text: Any amounts in the Guarantee Fund determined by the Secretary to be in excess of amounts currently required to carry out this section may be invested in obligations of the United States.

children.heading: Limitation on commitments to guarantee loans and mortgages
children.label: 5 Limitation on commitments to guarantee loans and mortgages

children.heading: Requirement of appropriations
children.label: A Requirement of appropriations
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section shall be effective for any fiscal year to the extent or in such amounts as are or have been provided in appropriations Acts, without regard to the fiscal year for which such amounts were appropriated.

children.heading: Limitations on costs of guarantees
children.label: B Limitations on costs of guarantees
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section shall be effective for any fiscal year only to the extent that amounts in the Guarantee Fund are or have been made available in appropriation Acts to cover the costs (as such term is defined in ) of such loan guarantees for such fiscal year. Any amounts appropriated pursuant to this subparagraph shall remain available until expended.section 661a of title 2

children.heading: Limitation on outstanding aggregate principal amount
children.label: C Limitation on outstanding aggregate principal amount
children.text: Subject to the limitations in subparagraphs (A) and (B), the Secretary may enter into commitments to guarantee loans under this section in each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012 with an aggregate outstanding principal amount not exceeding such amount as may be provided in appropriation Acts for such fiscal year.

children.heading: Liabilities
children.label: 6 Liabilities
children.text: All liabilities and obligations of the assets credited to the Guarantee Fund under paragraph (2)(A) shall be liabilities and obligations of the Guarantee Fund.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Guarantee Fund to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.
be decent, safe, sanitary, and modest in size and design;
conform with applicable general construction standards for the region;

children.label: A
children.text: has the capacity to maintain a minimum temperature in the dwelling of 65 degrees Fahrenheit during the coldest weather in the area;

children.label: B
children.text: is safe to operate and maintain;

children.label: C
children.text: delivers a uniform distribution of heat; and

children.label: D
children.text: conforms to any applicable tribal heating code or, if there is no applicable tribal code, an appropriate county, State, or National code;

children.label: A
children.text: uses a properly installed system of piping;

children.label: B
children.text: includes a kitchen sink and a partitional bathroom with lavatory, toilet, and bath or shower; and

children.label: C
children.text: uses water supply, plumbing, and sewage disposal systems that conform to any applicable tribal code or, if there is no applicable tribal code, the minimum standards established by the applicable county or State;

contain an electrical system using wiring and equipment properly installed to safely supply electrical energy for adequate lighting and for operation of appliances that conforms to any applicable tribal code or, if there is no applicable tribal code, an appropriate county, State, or National code;

children.label: i
children.text: 570 square feet in size, if designed for a family of not more than 4 persons;

children.label: ii
children.text: 850 square feet in size, if designed for a family of not less than 5 and not more than 7 persons; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1020 square feet in size, if designed for a family of not less than 8 persons, or

children.label: B
children.text: the size provided under the applicable locally adopted standards for size of dwelling units;
conform with the energy performance requirements for new construction established by the Secretary under section 526(a) of the National Housing Act [].12 U.S.C. 1735f–4(a)
be treated as a grant under the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 4101
be subject to the regulations promulgated by the Secretary to carry out section 105 of the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ().25 U.S.C. 4115
The term “family” means 1 or more persons maintaining a household, as the Secretary shall by regulation provide.
The term “Guarantee Fund” means the Indian Housing Loan Guarantee Fund established under subsection (i).
The term “Indian” means person recognized as being Indian or Alaska Native by an Indian tribe, the Federal Government, or any State.
The term “Indian area” means the area within which an Indian housing authority or Indian tribe is authorized to provide housing.

children.label: i
children.text: low-income housing for Indians; or

children.label: ii
children.text: housing subject to the provisions of this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: by exercise of the power of self-government of an Indian tribe independent of State law; or

children.label: ii
children.text: by operation of State law providing specifically for housing authorities for Indians, including regional housing authorities in the State of Alaska.
The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.
The term “standard housing” means a dwelling unit or housing that complies with the requirements established under subsection (j).

children.heading: .—Tribe; indian tribe
children.label: 8 .—Tribe; indian tribe
children.text: The term “tribe” or “Indian tribe” means any Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community of Indians, including any Alaska Native village or regional or village corporation as defined in or established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.], that is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians pursuant to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act of 1975 [ et seq.].43 U.S.C. 160125 U.S.C. 5301

The term “trust land” means land title to which is held by the United States for the benefit of an Indian or Indian tribe or title to which is held by an Indian tribe subject to a restriction against alienation imposed by the United States.
